content:
Character: Julian Reyes
Perception: Julian keeps different hours than I do, which is probably why it works. He's out the door before noon for prep, back long after I've closed out my last tab at The Goat. When our days do overlap — Mondays, the occasional Sunday — he cooks. Not for conversation, just because that's what he does. The smell alone makes the apartment feel like someone cares about it. He's not big on talking, doesn't press, doesn't perform. He fixed the kitchen faucet three weeks after I moved in without saying a word. That's the vocabulary he speaks: actions. I respect that more than I probably tell him. He reminds me of the good parts of home — the working-class kind of dependable that doesn't ask for applause. He carries something from back home, Chicago guilt probably, and I leave it there the way he leaves mine alone. Good roommate. Better person.
summary:

Perception: Julian keeps different hours than I do, which is probably why it works. He's out the door before noon for prep, back long after I've closed out my last tab at The Goat. When our days do overlap — Mondays, the occasional Sunday — he cooks. Not for conversation, just because that's what he does. The smell alone makes the apartment feel like someone cares about it. He's not big on talking, doesn't press, doesn't perform. He fixed the kitchen faucet three weeks after I moved in without saying a word. That's the vocabulary he speaks: actions. I respect that more than I probably tell him. He reminds me of the good parts of home — the working-class kind of dependable that doesn't ask for applause. He carries something from back home, Chicago guilt probably, and I leave it there the way he leaves mine alone. Good roommate. Better person.
